call_transcript: Agent: Hi Eleanor, this is Mark calling from Finance Solutions. I see you recently inquired about a personal loan on our website. Um, how are you doing today? Customer: Yeah, hi. Fine, I guess. Agent: Great! So, we're pre-approving you for a $10,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 8.99%.  Is that something you'd be interested in discussing? Customer: Well, I'm looking at consolidating some debt.  What are the terms like? Agent: Absolutely. The loan term is flexible, from 2 to 5 years.  We can tailor it to your monthly budget.  What kind of monthly payment are you comfortable with? Customer: I don't know, maybe around $200? Agent: Okay, let me run some numbers... With a 5-year term, your monthly payment would be approximately $198.  That includes the principal and interest.  There's a small origination fee of 1%, which is $100. Customer: Hmm, okay.  And there are no prepayment penalties, right? Agent: Correct, no prepayment penalties whatsoever.  You can pay it off early and save on interest.  So, does that sound good? Customer: Yeah, I think so. Let's do it. Agent: Fantastic! I just need to verify a few details...

Agent: Hello Mr. Miller? This is Sarah calling from Credit Solutions. We're offering a special promotion on our Signature Credit Card. Customer: Where did you get my number? I didn't give anyone permission to call me. Agent: Sir, we acquired your information through a marketing partner. We are pre-approving you for a $5,000 Signature Credit Card at an interest rate of 19.99%. Customer: 19.99?! That's outrageous! I already have a credit card. Agent: Well, sir, this card offers rewards points and a lower APR after the introductory period. Customer: Look, I'm retired. I don't need another credit card. And frankly, I'm not happy about being called. Agent: I understand, sir. I apologize for the inconvenience.  Is there anything else I can help you with? Customer: No. Just take my number off your list. *click*

Agent: Hi Jessica, this is David from Secure Finance. I'm calling regarding a referral from Northwest Bank.  How are you today? Customer: Hi, yeah, I was expecting your call.  I'm good. Agent: Excellent! So, we're pre-approving you for a $20,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 7.49%.  What are you hoping to use the funds for? Customer: I'm planning a home renovation.  Kitchen and bathroom. Agent: Wonderful!  We can definitely help with that.  The loan term can be anywhere from 3 to 7 years.  Let's look at a 5-year term. Your monthly payment would be around $380.  There's a $200 origination fee, and a late payment fee of $25. Customer: Okay, that sounds reasonable.  What about the APR? Agent: The APR is 7.99%, factoring in the origination fee.  We also offer a rate discount if you set up automatic payments. Customer: Oh, that's good to know.  I'd probably do that. Agent: Great!  So, we just need to verify your income and employment details...

Agent: Hello Mr. Brown? This is Lisa from Premier Credit. We have a fantastic offer on our Signature Credit Card. Customer: (Interrupting) Are you kidding me? You guys called me *yesterday*! I told you I'm not interested! Agent: Sir, I apologize for the repeated call. We are pre-approving you for a $7,500 Signature Credit Card at 17.99%. Customer: 17.99?! That's a joke! I'm on the do-not-call list! Agent: Sir, I assure you... Customer: (Interrupting) No, you assure *nothing*!  I want my number removed from your list *immediately*!  And I'm filing a complaint! *click*

Agent: Hi Maria, this is Tom calling from Fast Loans. I see you submitted an application for a personal loan online. How are you doing today? Customer: Hi, yeah, I did. I'm okay. Agent: Great! We're pre-approving you for an $8,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 9.49%. What are you planning to use the loan for? Customer: I need to pay for some medical bills. They're piling up. Agent: I'm sorry to hear that. We can definitely help. With a 3-year term, your monthly payment would be around $245. There's a $80 application fee. Customer: Okay, that sounds manageable. What's the process like? Agent: It's very straightforward. We just need to verify your income and identity. We'll send you a link to upload some documents. Customer: Alright. That sounds good. Agent: Wonderful! I'll send that link over right now.  Would you prefer email or text? Customer: Email is fine.

Agent: Hello Mr. Lee? This is Emily from Universal Credit. We're offering a special credit card... Customer: (Interrupting) Huh? What was that? Speak up, please. Agent: I said, we're offering a special credit card. We are pre-approving you for a $2,500 Signature Credit Card at 22.99%. Customer: 22.99? What does that even *mean*? I don't understand. Agent: It's the interest rate, sir. The percentage you pay on your balance. Customer: Oh. Oh dear. I... I think I need to talk to my daughter about this. No, thank you. *click*

Agent: Hi Ashley, this is Kevin from Prime Finance. I see you applied for a personal loan online. How are you doing? Customer: Hey, yeah, I did. I'm good. Just busy. Agent: Understandable. We're pre-approving you for a $15,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 12.5%. What are you planning to use the funds for? Customer: I want to buy a car. My old one just died. Agent: Excellent! We can definitely help with that. We have several loan terms available. A 5-year term would give you a monthly payment of around $330. There's a $150 processing fee. Customer: Okay. And what's the APR? Agent: The APR is 13.2%, including the processing fee. We also offer a discount if you enroll in automatic payments. Customer: Hmm. That's a little higher than I was hoping for. Agent: I understand. Let me see if I can adjust the term to lower your monthly payment. A 6-year term would bring it down to $280, but the APR would be slightly higher, at 13.5%. Customer: Okay, that's better. But is that the best rate you can offer? Agent: That's the best rate we can offer given your credit score. However, if you can provide proof of a co-signer with a higher credit score, we might be able to lower it further. Customer: I don't have a co-signer. Agent: Alright. Well, let's proceed with the 6-year term then. I'll send you the loan agreement to review...

Agent: Hi Brian, this is Sarah from Elite Finance. I'm calling as a valued customer. We have a special offer on our Signature Credit Card with a low introductory rate. Customer: Oh, hi Sarah. Yeah, I remember getting a referral from my bank. What's the offer? Agent: We're pre-approving you for a $10,000 Signature Credit Card at an interest rate of 6.99%. It also comes with travel rewards and purchase protection. Customer: That sounds pretty good. I've been wanting to travel more. What's the catch? Agent: No catch! It's a limited-time offer for our preferred customers. There's a $50 annual fee, but the rewards you earn will easily offset that. Customer: Okay. And what's the APR after the introductory period? Agent: The APR after the introductory period is 14.99%, but you can avoid it by maintaining a good credit history. Customer: Alright. I think I'm in. Agent: Fantastic! I just need to confirm a few details and then we can activate your card immediately. Can you verify your address for me?

Agent: Hi Linda, this is Michael from Secure Loans. I see you requested information about a personal loan on our website. How are you today? Customer: Hi, yeah, I did. I'm doing okay. Agent: Great! We're pre-approving you for a $12,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 10.99%. What are you planning to use the funds for? Customer: I'm consolidating some high-interest credit card debt. Agent: Excellent! That's a smart move. We can definitely help with that. We offer competitive rates and flexible terms. Have you been shopping around for other loans? Customer: Yeah, I got a quote from First National Bank. They offered me 11.5%. Agent: Ah, okay. Well, our rate is lower, and we also have a faster approval process. Plus, we don't charge any hidden fees. There's a $120 origination fee, but that's it. Customer: Hmm. That's good. What's the difference between your loan and a balance transfer to a credit card? Agent: That's a great question. A balance transfer usually has a limited introductory period, and then the rate goes up. Our loan has a fixed rate for the entire term, so you know exactly what your payments will be. Customer: Okay, that makes sense. Agent: So, are you interested in proceeding with the application?

Agent: Hello Mr. O'Connell? This is Lisa from Credit Solutions. We're offering a special credit card... Customer: (Interrupting) Look, I'm unemployed. I don't need a credit card. Where did you even get my number? *click*
